scanner_blind_spots.reason: Scanner missed because Eastern European IT services look geo-risky; CEO insider buy ($2M+) and sub-10x P/E on recovering revenue is high-conviction value not visible in automated screens
Scanner may have flagged high P/E without seeing the CFO's first-ever Form 4 buy or Ackman 13G filing — qualitative insider signals require human synthesis
Scanner screens filter out high P/E; missed the $10B Army Enterprise AI contract and CEO $50M buy — the government AI OS thesis requires reading primary contract announcements
Scanner flagged defense but missed RTX specifically; earnings beat+raise sold off on tariff noise that doesn't apply (95% US-sourced) creates a classic sentiment vs. fundamentals dislocation
Automated screens don't aggregate Form 4s to detect cluster buys; 10 director/executive purchases within a 6-week window at $280-295 is a classic 'management knows the MCR trough' signal
Howmet's dual-market position (aerospace + data center power) isn't visible in single-sector screens; pre-announced beat on April 18 before this scan wasn't captured
Discovery caught the NVIDIA preferred partner announcement for Rubin cooling — this required reading GTC 2026 session notes that post-date most scanner data refreshes

value_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: EU parliament voted May 2025 to ban Russian LNG imports by 2027; Hormuz closure scenarios elevate LNG spot pricing; Cheniere signed multi-year offtake with multiple European buyers in Q1 2026 (Tier 4 sources)

Discovery: The 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis (blockade effective February 28, 2026, US-Israel air war on Iran) removed ~20% of global LNG supply; European gas prices surged 60-85%, the EU banned Russian LNG spot cargoes on April 25, and Cheniere set a US export record in March while simultaneously completing a new Corpus Christi terminal expansion — yet the stock is only up ~10%, significantly underpricing a structural long-term supply shift to US LNG.
value_picks.analyst_take: Strait of Hormuz closure risk + EU legislating ban on Russian LNG = structural demand surge for Sabine Pass/Corpus Christi capacity; EU paying spot premium for non-Russian supply

value_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Golden Dome program $185B revised cost benefits PAC-3 (LMT) and THAAD systems; $838.7B FY2026 defense budget; LMT Q1 beat with raised guidance; Iran conflict driving active procurement (Tier 2-4 sources)

Discovery: Defense prime contractor down ~26% from 52-week high ($692 → ~$513) — barely blood-in-the-streets territory but uncommon for an $838.7B US defense budget year with ongoing Iran conflict generating active procurement demand; $194B record backlog provides multi-year visibility, and specific PAC-3/THAAD Congressional line items directly benefit LMT; bear case is fixed-price contract margin pressure (cited by analysts), and 'crowded trade correcting' risk if geopolitical tension unexpectedly de-escalates.
value_picks.analyst_take: Cheapest defense prime at 17x fwd P/E with $194B record backlog; Golden Dome PAC-3/THAAD Congressional line items; -26% from 52W high in record-defense-budget year
